page.title: Partner portal for a B2B company | Bc. Ondrej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Partner portal for a B2B company and shared operational visibility
page.description: A partner-portal use case for B2B companies that need one place for documents, requests, collaboration status, and self-service actions for distributors, resellers, or business partners.

hero.title: One place for partners instead of email threads and manual follow-up
hero.subtitle: Useful when partners repeatedly need documents, request status, order context, or guided next steps connected to the company's internal process.

intro: A partner portal is a strong use case where the company is not just serving one-off client requests but supporting ongoing work with distributors, commercial partners, or a partner network.

The strongest first version usually starts with a few scenarios that have the biggest impact on partner experience and internal operations rather than trying to digitise the whole relationship at once.

sections.title: When this type of solution makes sense
body: This is not a universal template. It is a representative model of situations where a similar system makes clear commercial and operational sense.
bullets: partners repeatedly need status visibility for requests, orders, or service cases
documents, pricing, or support materials are still shared manually
the company wants self-service steps for partners without extra internal overhead
commercial or operations teams spend too much time on repetitive partner support

sections.title: What the solution usually includes
body: The exact scope varies by company, but similar patterns repeat around roles, workflow, data boundaries, and ownership.
bullets: partner accounts, roles, and access boundaries
status visibility for requests, collaboration, or orders
document handling, notifications, and next-step actions
integration with internal systems, CRM, or back-office workflow

sections.title: What the system should improve
body: The point is not merely replacing one tool with another. The important part is reducing friction, clarifying state, and lowering avoidable manual work.
bullets: less manual partner support and follow-up
greater trust in the operational and service process
a stronger base for future digital partner services

faq.question: Is a partner portal different from a client portal?
faq.answer: Often yes. Partner collaboration usually has different roles, permissions, and a longer operational relationship with the internal process.

faq.question: Does the portal need to cover every partner scenario from day one?
faq.answer: No. It is usually better to start with the few interactions that reduce support load and improve clarity fastest.

faq.question: Can the portal connect to existing internal systems?
faq.answer: Yes. The link to internal data and workflow is usually what makes the portal genuinely useful.

for: B2B companies working with recurring partner interactions
teams trying to reduce support overhead around partners
situations where the portal clearly connects to internal process and data
notFor: portals with no connection to internal systems
projects with no owner for the partner workflow

cta.label: Explore the project fit
cta.note: A short summary of the workflow, users, and current friction is enough to assess the fit.
